title: Objects, Packfiles, Reachability, and Integrity · Graph Retrieval
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs objects, packfiles, reachability, and integrity from terse repository evidence, challenges one retained misconception, and revises the object-database integrity and reachability report. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for object-database integrity and reachability report after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace blobs, trees, commits, annotated tags, parent edges, and reference roots as an immutable reachable object graph.
Explain loose objects, pack indexes, delta bases, multi-pack indexes, and why storage representation does not change object identity.
Distinguish reachable, unreachable, dangling, promised, missing, and corrupt objects using fsck and reference evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle
steps.instruction: Order the Gita Object Graph Invariants evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Gita Object Graph Invariants assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Case G41 is a review in a disposable repository. the reported tree is unreachable but still present rather than corrupt or promised. The learner must predict fsck classifies connectivity and names candidate unreachable objects without pruning them, run git fsck --full --no-reflogs --unreachable, explain the candidate tree is present and reachable from the retained incident commit only, test this changed condition: remove the retained incident root in the simulator and observe the commit become unreachable, and preserve this recovery boundary: create refs/recovery/integrity before any repack expiration or prune operation.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Gita Object Graph Invariants has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Gita Object Graph Invariants precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Case G41 is a review in a disposable repository. the reported tree is unreachable but still present rather than corrupt or promised. The learner must predict fsck classifies connectivity and names candidate unreachable objects without pruning them, run git fsck --full --no-reflogs --unreachable, explain the candidate tree is present and reachable from the retained incident commit only, test this changed condition: remove the retained incident root in the simulator and observe the commit become unreachable, and preserve this recovery boundary: create refs/recovery/integrity before any repack expiration or prune operation.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A commit contains file contents directly instead of naming a root tree.

options.text: Inspect repository boundary, config origin, current operation, worktree, index, refs, and graph
Preserve uncommitted work and a recovery ref, then state intended tree and topology
Preview and perform the least destructive simulated Git operation
Verify status, staged and range diffs, graph, tests, remote target, and recovery path

stimulus.title: Gita Integrity Preservation case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Case G47 is a review in a disposable repository. the reported tree is unreachable but still present rather than corrupt or promised. The learner must predict fsck classifies connectivity and names candidate unreachable objects without pruning them, run git fsck --full --no-reflogs --unreachable, explain the candidate tree is present and reachable from the retained incident commit only, test this changed condition: remove the retained incident root in the simulator and observe the commit become unreachable, and preserve this recovery boundary: create refs/recovery/integrity before any repack expiration or prune operation.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Running gc is an appropriate first repair for a missing-object report.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Quarantine suspect storage, preserve alternate copies and refs, verify object connectivity, and defer destructive pruning until recovery proof exists. Artifact under review: object-database integrity and reachability report.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Gita Integrity Preservation, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write the review evidence note for Gita Integrity Preservation: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Case G48 is a review in a disposable repository. the reported tree is unreachable but still present rather than corrupt or promised. The learner must predict fsck classifies connectivity and names candidate unreachable objects without pruning them, run git fsck --full --no-reflogs --unreachable, explain the candidate tree is present and reachable from the retained incident commit only, test this changed condition: remove the retained incident root in the simulator and observe the commit become unreachable, and preserve this recovery boundary: create refs/recovery/integrity before any repack expiration or prune operation.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Quarantine suspect storage, preserve alternate copies and refs, verify object connectivity, and defer destructive pruning until recovery proof exists.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The decision applies Gita Object Graph Invariants to the changed stakeholder case.
